title: ALP board configuration (board.yaml)
description: Schema for a project's board.yaml -- the single configuration file a firmware project supplies.  Describes the board the firmware targets: its name, the SoM mounted on it, the firmware running on each core, the chips populated on the board, and the E1M-pad routing.  Pin + populated declarations are either INLINE (`populated:` + `e1m_routes:` at top level) or by REFERENCE (`preset:` pointing at a shared board definition under `metadata/boards/<preset>.yaml`).  Mixing inline and preset is rejected.

allOf.description: `preset:` is mutually exclusive with inline `name:` / `populated:` / `e1m_routes:`.  Projects without any board declaration (headless / inference-only builds) are also valid -- they get no chip drivers and no E1M-pad routes header.
Inline board declarations with board-side population or E1M routes need a stable `name:` for generated route headers and diagnostics.

name.description: Board name.  Required when the board is defined inline (no `preset:`); used in the generated `include/alp/boards/alp_<name>_routes.h` filename + as the `@brief` board identity in Doxygen.  When `preset:` is used the field is forbidden -- the resolved preset's `name:` carries the board identity.

description.description: Free-form one-line description of this project / board.

preset.description: SDK-internal shortcut: reference to a shared board definition at `metadata/boards/<preset>.yaml`.  Used by the SDK's own EVK demo projects under `examples/` to share the EVK + X-EVK definitions without copy-paste.  Customer projects write the board out inline (`name:` + `populated:` + `e1m_routes:` at the top level) -- they don't need to use `preset:`.  When set, the preset's `name`, `populated`, `e1m_routes`, `default_hw_rev`, and `hw_revisions` blocks are pulled in wholesale; mutually exclusive with inline `populated:` / `e1m_routes:`.

hw_rev.description: Board hardware revision.  Optional; the SDK falls back to the preset's `default_hw_rev` (preset mode) or treats the board as unrevisioned (inline mode).  Distinct from `som.hw_rev` which versions the SoM module itself.

som.description: SoM identity -- the *module* the board mounts.  On-module components (silicon, on-module radio, secure element, on-module sensors) are fixed at SoM-fab time and cannot be DNI'd after order.

sku.description: Stock SoM SKU (MPN).  Resolves to a preset at metadata/e1m_modules/<MPN>.yaml.  Recognised: E1M-AEN301/401/501/601/701/801 (Alif Ensemble), E1M-V2N101/V2N102 (Renesas RZ/V2N), E1M-V2M101/V2M102 (V2N + DEEPX), E1M-NX9xxx (NXP i.MX 93; E1M-NX9101 is a placeholder preset until the production MPN lands).

hw_rev.description: SoM module hardware revision.  Optional; the SDK falls back to the SKU preset's `default_hw_rev` when omitted.

cores.description: Per-core runtime + app mapping.  Keys are canonical core IDs from the SoM preset's `topology:` (e.g. `a55_cluster`, `m33_sm`, `m55_hp`, `m55_he`, `a32_cluster`, `m33`).  The orchestrator fans out one slice per non-`off` entry.  Customer may declare a subset; unspecified cores inherit the preset's `topology:` defaults.  Across-SoM-family swaps (AEN <-> V2N) require updating these keys -- per ADR-0011 cross-family portability is explicitly not a goal.

populated.description: Inline mode only.  Board-side chip-driver / block-helper population.  Keys match chip-driver / block names; each `true` enables the corresponding CONFIG_ALP_SDK_{CHIP,BLOCK}_<name>=y.  Mutually exclusive with `preset:`.

e1m_routes.description: Inline mode only.  Authoritative board-side E1M-pad routing.  scripts/gen_board_header.py reads this block and emits include/alp/boards/alp_<name>_routes.h.  Mutually exclusive with `preset:`.

oneOf.description: Bare E1M pad name (loader looks up the matching macro + doc from the resolved board).

macro.description: C macro the project's source references for this pad (must match the resolved board's `e1m_routes:` entry for the same pad).

doc.description: One-line human-readable label for this pad as the project uses it.  Optional; the loader falls back to the resolved board's `doc:` when omitted.

pins.description: E1M pads / peripheral instances the project actively uses.  Each entry is either a bare E1M pad name (e.g. `E1M_GPIO_IO4`) or a `{e1m, macro?, doc?}` object that pins the C macro the source references + a one-line label.  The loader cross-checks every entry against the resolved board's `e1m_routes:` block: the `e1m` must exist, and when `macro:` is supplied it must match the board's macro for that pad.  Bare-string and object entries can mix in the same list.  Optional even when `preset:` is set.

ipc.description: Cross-core IPC carve-outs.  Each entry declares a shared-memory region the orchestrator allocates from the SoM preset's `memory_map:` regions whose `accessible_from:` covers every listed endpoint.  Carve-out addresses are computed deterministically and recorded in the system manifest.

diagnostics.description: Project-wide diagnostics configuration (applies to every slice).

last_error.description: Thread-local alp_last_error() slot.  Disable to save a few bytes of TLS on extremely-tight builds.

log_level.description: Default log level for every Alp SDK module that does not appear in `modules:` below.

console.description: Console backend the loader wires per slice.  `auto` (default) selects by the slice OS: a Zephyr slice gets the Alp UART console (`alp`/`uart` -- printf lands on the module console UART, e.g. E1M edge UART0), a Yocto slice gets the Linux console.  `ram` forces the Zephyr RAM console for serial-less bench boards (read `ram_console_buf` over SWD).  `none` emits no console Kconfig and inherits the board default.  The board layer owns the physical console UART (`zephyr,console` DT chosen); this knob only picks the backend.

modules.description: Per-module log-level overrides.  Keys match Alp SDK module names (e.g. `alp_iot`, `alp_inference`, `alp_audio`, `alp_security`, `alp_gpio`, ...).  Each value is the same log-level enum as the top-level `log_level`, plus `off` to silence the module entirely.  The loader emits CONFIG_<MODULE>_LOG_LEVEL_<LEVEL>=y per entry into the generated alp.conf.

storage.description: Optional flash / persistent-storage partitioning.  Each entry declares one partition: a name, a filesystem (or `raw: true` for unformatted partitions used as OTA slot backing / image cache), a size, an optional mount point, and a flash-device reference into the SoM preset's `memory_map:` or `on_module.ospi_memories:` block.  The orchestrator emits a partial DTS overlay (the `&<flash>; partitions { ... }` node) plus the matching Kconfig (CONFIG_FILE_SYSTEM_LITTLEFS / CONFIG_FAT_FILESYSTEM_ELM / ...) per entry.  Partition addresses are pinned by the orchestrator (bottom-up, page-aligned within the flash device) for byte-stable OTA images across rebuilds; supply `offset_kib:` to override the allocator with an explicit offset.

name.description: Partition label; becomes the DT node name + CONFIG_FS_LITTLEFS_PARTITION_<NAME> stem.

fs.description: Filesystem to format the partition with.  `raw` leaves it unformatted (e.g. for image slots managed by MCUboot).

mount.description: Mount point at boot.  Optional; when omitted the partition isn't auto-mounted.

flash_device.description: Region this partition lives in.  References the SoM preset's `memory_map:` name (`mram_main`, `ocram_low`, ...) or an `on_module.ospi_memories:` entry (`ospi0`, ...).

offset_kib.description: Optional explicit offset (in KiB) within the flash device.  When omitted, the orchestrator allocates bottom-up from offset 0 in name-sorted order.  When supplied, must be page-aligned (4 KiB); the allocator surfaces overlaps via `status: blocked` on the resolved partition.

raw.description: Legacy alias for `fs: raw`.  Prefer `fs:` directly.

security.description: Optional security-stack configuration.  Today covers the PSA Crypto persistent-key store + TF-M boundary; future blocks (secure-boot key provisioning, attestation enrolment) land here.

psa.description: PSA Crypto persistent-key storage.  See docs/security-audit-plan.md and ADR-0010 + ADR-0013 for the cross-core trust-boundary discussion.  When `tfm: true` the orchestrator emits a TF-M sysbuild child-image overlay at `build/sysbuild/tfm/tfm.conf` (SB_CONFIG_TFM + CONFIG_PSA_CRYPTO_*); `tfm: false` keeps PSA Crypto entirely non-secure (mbedTLS-only).

persistent_slots.description: Number of PSA Crypto persistent key slots (emits CONFIG_PSA_CRYPTO_PERSISTENT_SLOT_COUNT).

its_storage.description: ITS (Internal Trusted Storage) backing region.  References the SoM preset's `memory_map:` name -- typically `mram_secure` on AEN, `ocram_secure` on V2N.

ps_storage.description: PS (Protected Storage) backing region.  Optional; references an on-module OSPI / EEPROM region when the customer wants encrypted-at-rest persistence outside the secure MRAM.

tfm.description: Enable the TF-M secure-side partition image.  When true the orchestrator emits SB_CONFIG_TFM_BUILD_TYPE + a sysbuild child image; when false PSA Crypto runs entirely non-secure (mbedTLS-only).

attestation_root.description: Source of the attestation root key.  `optiga_trust_m` uses the on-module secure element (AEN family); `tfm_internal` keeps the root in TF-M's secure storage; `none` disables attestation.

boot.description: Optional bootloader + image-signing configuration.  When absent, SDK defaults apply (AEN-Zephyr: MCUboot + ECDSA-P256 + swap-using-scratch + 480 KiB slots; Yocto SoMs: U-Boot inherited from machine.conf).  Project-wide (not per-core): the bootloader is a device-level artifact, not a per-OS choice.  See docs/secure-boot.md.

method.description: Bootloader strategy.  `mcuboot` pulls MCUboot into the Zephyr build via sysbuild; `none` disables the bootloader (firmware runs unverified -- dev / lab only).  Optional; the SoM family supplies the default (AEN/N93 -> mcuboot, V2N/V2N-M1 -> none on the Zephyr slice since U-Boot owns boot on Linux).

signing.description: Image-signing configuration.  Required when method is `mcuboot`.

algorithm.description: Image signature algorithm.  Must match the SoM's hardware constraints (e.g. OPTIGA Trust M slot type on AEN).

key_file.description: Public key file (PEM) relative to the project root.  Baked into the bootloader; the private half stays in OPTIGA / dev-keys / vault.

slots.description: Dual-bank image partition layout.  Sizes validated against the SoM preset's `memory_map:` at load time.

swap_algorithm.description: MCUboot swap strategy.  `scratch` (crash-safe, needs scratch partition), `move` (more erases, no scratch), `overwrite` (no rollback).

scratch_size_kib.description: Scratch partition size (only when swap_algorithm is `scratch`).

anti_rollback.description: Enable monotonic image counters.  Requires OTP fuse provisioning at production time (see docs/secure-boot.md §anti-rollback).

build_type.description: Build flavour propagated to sysbuild child images (MCUboot + TF-M).  Set to `Debug` for symbol-rich dev builds; `MinSizeRel` matches the upstream sysbuild template's size-optimised default.

ota.description: Optional OTA configuration.  Project-wide (one device = one OTA orchestration).  See docs/ota.md for the device contract.  Yocto path: Mender; Zephyr path: deferred until the SDK lands its in-tree OTA client (Mender MCU client or Hawkbit, decision pending per ADR 0009).

provider.description: OTA provider (provider-driven dispatch, ADR 0009 follow-up).  `mender` -> server-mode Mender on Yocto OR Mender-MCU-client on Zephyr; `hawkbit` -> Zephyr upstream's Hawkbit DDI client; `mcumgr` -> Zephyr's MCUmgr/SMP (transport selection stays the app's call); `none` to disable.

signing_key.description: Path to the public key / cert (relative to project root) used to verify OTA artifacts.  Private key never lives here.

tls_ca_bundle.description: Optional pinned CA bundle for production.  Path relative to project root.

enabled.description: Automatic rollback on health-check failure after update.

min_version.description: Anti-downgrade floor.  Reject OTA images claiming version < min_version.

poll_interval_s.description: How often the device polls the deployment server for updates.  Mender default is 60 s; SDK default is 1800 s (30 min) for battery-friendly nodes.

storage.description: Linux/Yocto-only: A/B rootfs partition geometry.  Ignored on Zephyr (MCUboot is slot-based).

chips.description: Chip drivers the application links directly via <alp/chips/<name>.h>.  Each entry enables the matching CONFIG_ALP_SDK_CHIP_<NAME>=y.  Shared across cores (project-wide chip population).

libraries.description: Curated third-party libraries to pull into the project (ADR 0018).  Each entry names a manifest under metadata/libraries/<name>.yaml; the orchestrator emits its per-OS wiring (Zephyr Kconfig / Yocto IMAGE_INSTALL / baremetal CMake) through the --emit contract and rejects a selection incompatible with the target SoM/core.  Project-wide: applied to every core whose OS the manifest supports.  Distinct from the per-core `cores.<id>.libraries:` curated-token list.

features.description: Typed feature configuration with explicit loader/emitter support.  Unsupported feature keys are rejected so board.yaml cannot silently accept a block no backend consumes.

hw_info.description: Runtime hardware-info reader configuration emitted into the system manifest and per-slice build config.

eeprom.description: EEPROM location of the 128-byte alp_hw_info_eeprom_t manifest.

bus.description: Portable E1M I2C bus slug (for example, e1m_i2c0).  The emitter resolves the trailing index to CONFIG_ALP_SDK_HW_INFO_EEPROM_I2C_BUS_ID.

offset.description: Byte offset of the alp_hw_info_eeprom_t manifest inside the EEPROM.

models.description: AI models to compile + package into .alpmodel (silicon-agnostic; backends derived from som.sku).

source.description: Path (relative to board.yaml) to the source model. `.tflite` compiles for CPU (TFLM) + Ethos-U (Vela) out of the box; `.onnx` requires a vendor toolchain (DRP-AI / DEEPX) on the build host and is otherwise recorded as a coverage skip.

spec.description: Optional $ref to an external model spec for large/shared models.

compile.description: Per-backend compile configuration for NPU toolchains that need a per-model config + calibration the SDK cannot derive (DRP-AI, DEEPX). Keys are backend ids; a backend with no block here is recorded as a coverage skip ("no compile config") rather than guessed. Paths are relative to this board.yaml.

config.description: Path to the dxcom JSON config (per-model quantization/compile settings).

calibration.description: Path to the calibration dataset directory for post-training quantization.

spec.description: Path to the DRP-AI TVM compile spec (model input/quant configuration).

supported_boards.description: Board presets this project/example is verified to build for (e.g. [e1m-evk, e1m-x-evk]). Examples only; per ADR-0011 application firmware still targets one SoM family. Twister builds one scenario per entry. Defaults to the single `preset:` when omitted.

doc.description: One-line Doxygen @brief documenting what the pad is wired to on this board.

active_low.description: Board convention -- pressed/asserted = 0 V.  Documentation only; the app passes ALP_GPIO_ACTIVE_LOW at alp_gpio_open() time if it wants auto-inversion.

pull.description: Physical pull-resistor present on the PCB (board-static fact).  Apps still pass the matching ALP_GPIO_PULL_{UP,DOWN} at alp_gpio_open() time -- this field documents what the board ships with, it does not drive the controller's internal pulls.

debounce_ms.description: RC-debounce time constant in milliseconds for the board's hardware debounce (mostly buttons).  Documentation hint; the app may add software debounce on top.

board_alias.description: Portable cross-EVK alias (e1m-spec STANDARD.md §7.2 common role). gen_board_header.py emits `#define <board_alias> <macro>` into the generated routes header. The SAME board_alias MUST be declared on every board that exposes the role (enforced by tests/scripts/test_board_alias_parity.py).

route_entry_list_gpio.description: GPIO routes.  Accepts the IO pads (E1M_GPIO_IO<N>) and the GPIO-secondary index of any analog/timer pad (E1M_GPIO_PWM<N> / ADC<N> / DAC<N> / ENC<N>_<X|Y>).  Per the e1m-spec every digital pad has a universal GPIO secondary, so e.g. a PWM-pad LED driven as a plain digital GPIO is routed here via E1M_GPIO_PWM<N> (distinct from its E1M_PWM<N> entry in the `pwm:` section).

core_entry.description: Per-core runtime + app slice.  Mirrors the SoM preset's `topology.<id>` shape, with the addition of per-slice app/library/peripheral/inference/iot settings.  EVERY field is optional and inherits from the SoM preset's `topology.<core_id>` defaults when omitted; the customer writes only what differs.

os.description: The OS runtime is DERIVED from the core's silicon class and is not selectable: Cortex-M -> Zephyr (RTOS), Cortex-A -> Yocto (Linux). Omit `os:` to take that runtime. The only values a board.yaml may set explicitly are `off` (skip the slice; no app required) or `baremetal` (rare hand-written firmware in place of the core's RTOS). Setting the OTHER class's OS (e.g. `zephyr` on a Cortex-A, `yocto` on a Cortex-M) is rejected by the loader -- the enum lists every resolved runtime, but the cross-file core-class check (board.yaml `os:` vs the SoC `cores[].type`) is enforced in scripts/alp_orchestrate.py, since JSON Schema cannot see the SoC spec.

app.description: Path to the app source directory for this core.  Required for `os: zephyr` (must contain prj.conf or CMakeLists.txt), `os: baremetal` (must contain CMakeLists.txt).  For `os: yocto` either `app:` (custom CMake project recipe) or `image:` (override the stock recipe).

image.description: Yocto image recipe name (e.g. `alp-image-edge`).  Used when `os: yocto` and no custom `app:` is supplied.

peripherals.description: Peripheral subsystems this slice uses directly via the matching <alp/<class>.h> wrapper.  Each entry enables the corresponding Zephyr Kconfig / Yocto package at build time.

libraries.description: Libraries available to this slice's app code.  The SDK adds the library's include path + the matching compile-time profile.

extra_libraries.description: Open-set escape hatch for libraries the SDK doesn't curate.  Each entry must declare inline `kconfig:` OR a `profile:` file path -- exactly one (enforced by the loader).  Use this when you need to pull in a library that isn't part of the curated `libraries:` enum (a one-off vendor SDK, a research-only dep, a soon-to-be-curated lib while the profile lands).  Inline `kconfig:` is the fast path; `profile:` points at a `hw-backends.yaml`-style file for libraries that want per-silicon backend selection consistent with the curated entries.

name.description: Unique slug.  Loader enforces global uniqueness across every core's `extra_libraries:` and rejects collisions with the curated `libraries:` enum.

include_path.description: Optional include search path (repo-relative or absolute) the build adds for this library's headers.

kconfig.description: Inline Kconfig fragment lines emitted verbatim into alp.conf (e.g. `CONFIG_MYLIB=y`).  Mutually exclusive with `profile:`.

profile.description: Path (repo-relative) to a hw-backends.yaml-style profile file with `accelerators:`/`sw_fallback:` blocks.  Loader applies the same silicon / soc_family / requires_cap matching used by the curated libraries.  Mutually exclusive with `kconfig:`.

memory.description: Per-slice memory tuning.  Optional; the SoC's Kconfig defaults apply when omitted.

stack_kib.description: Main thread stack size in KiB.  Zephyr: emits CONFIG_MAIN_STACK_SIZE=<bytes> into the slice's alp.conf.  Yocto: ignored (kernel-side stack sized by the kernel).

heap_kib.description: Heap pool size in KiB.  Zephyr: emits CONFIG_HEAP_MEM_POOL_SIZE=<bytes>.  Yocto: ignored.

isr_stack_kib.description: ISR stack size in KiB.  Zephyr only; emits CONFIG_ISR_STACK_SIZE.

power.description: Per-slice power-management profile.  Optional; conservative defaults apply when omitted.  Maps to the SoM's PMIC + Zephyr PM subsystem knobs.

sleep_mode.description: Lowest sleep state the slice is allowed to enter.  Zephyr: enables CONFIG_PM + CONFIG_PM_DEVICE; selects the corresponding PM_STATE_* hierarchy.  `disabled` = always-on (default); `deep` = lowest-power retention state the silicon supports.

wakeup_sources.description: Peripherals / IRQ lines allowed to wake the slice from sleep.  Each entry is a peripheral subsystem name (`uart`, `gpio`, `rtc`, `i2c`, ...) or an `E1M_*` pad name.  The loader maps to the matching `CONFIG_PM_DEVICE_WAKE_*` Kconfig per silicon family.

inference.description: Per-slice inference tuning.  Backend selection is NOT a field here -- silicon capability is dictated by the SoM preset's `capabilities:` matrix and the SDK compiles in every dispatcher the SoM supports.  Apps pick per-handle at runtime via alp_inference_open(.backend=...), enabling concurrent multi-NPU dispatch on V2M101 (DRP-AI3 + DEEPX DX-M1).  This block carries app-level tuning only (memory arena size).

default_arena_kib.description: Per-model TFLM scratch arena size in KiB.  App-level memory budget; not silicon-determined.

iot.description: Connectivity feature toggles for this slice.

kind.description: IPC channel kind.  `rpmsg` rides OpenAMP; `raw_shmem` is plain shared memory + custom synchronisation; `mailbox_only` is doorbell + signal only (no shared memory).

endpoints.description: Core IDs participating in this channel.  Must all be present in `cores:` with `os != off`.

carve_out_kb.description: Shared-memory region size in KiB requested for this channel.  The orchestrator allocates from the SoM preset's `memory_map:` regions whose `accessible_from:` covers every endpoint.

name.description: Channel identifier (e.g. `alp_default_rpmsg`).  Used as the carve-out label in DT reservations + as the RPMsg name-service tag.

cacheable.description: Override the default cacheable hint.  Default carve-out is non-cacheable (cf. spec §6.8).

address.description: Explicit base address override.  Normally the orchestrator allocates an address from the memory map; setting this pins the carve-out.
